title: spawn_scanner silently swallows scan errors — no backoff, no reconnect
description: spawn_scanner logs Err from scan_to_tip at warn level then immediately retries after poll_interval. If get_block_range fails mid-stream (gRPC channel broken, TLS reset), the same LightwalletdClient is reused for the next iteration — but client.rs says 'Reconnection is the caller's responsibility: if a streaming RPC returns an error, drop this client and call connect again. The compact-block scanner owns the reconnect loop.' spawn_scanner does NOT reconnect: it calls s.scan_to_tip() in a loop on the same scanner, which holds the same client. After a gRPC error the next scan_to_tip will call client.latest_height() on the broken channel, fail again, and this repeats forever with no reconnection attempt.
